findings.description: .env.example documents LLM_PROVIDER=openai as a valid option and OPENAI_LLM_MODEL is annotated 'used when LLM_PROVIDER=openai or hybrid', but the factory match statement only has 'anthropic' and 'hybrid' cases. Setting LLM_PROVIDER=openai raises 'Unknown LLM provider: openai'. Either add an 'openai' case that returns a standalone OpenAILLMAdapter or remove 'openai' from the documented options in .env.example.

test_extract_menu_data_returns_items mocks the OpenAI tool_call with items using key 'item_name', but EXTRACT_MENU_SCHEMA (_tool_schemas.py:80-87) declares the required key as 'name'. The adapter passes payload.get('items', []) through unchanged, so the test passes against an incorrect shape. This locks in a silent divergence: a real API response matching the schema ('name') would fail the test assertion at line 146, and a response matching the test ('item_name') would violate the declared tool schema. Fix the mock to use 'name' (and any consumer code that expects 'name').

classify_photo prompt omits the 'If both MENU and VIBE apply, choose MENU' disambiguation rule that anthropic_adapter.py:251 includes. The eval gate measures classify_photo_agreement between the two providers; by construction a missing tie-breaker rule guarantees disagreement on menu+vibe edge cases and will depress the metric. Share the full prompt text between adapters (hoist to a module-level constant or use the SYSTEM_PROMPT pattern).

N+1 DB query pattern: the --auto branch already fetches the full shop rows with the same shop_fields list at lines 89-98, but the main loop at line 121 immediately re-queries each shop individually via db.table('shops').select(shop_fields).eq('id', sid).limit(1). Violates CLAUDE.md Performance Standards ('No N+1 queries — use JOINs or IN()') and 'batch calls at end, not per-item'. Reuse the rows already in memory when shop_ids came from --auto, or batch-fetch with .in_('id', shop_ids) once.

_build_enrich_messages declares parameters menu_vocab_ref and specialty_vocab_ref but never uses them — the function imports _MENU_VOCAB_REF / _SPECIALTY_VOCAB_REF directly from providers.llm.anthropic_adapter inside the function body. Call site in enrich_shop passes empty strings for both. Drop the dead parameters and hoist the import to module scope.

assign_tarot imports TAROT_TITLES, TITLE_TO_TAGS from core.tarot_vocabulary inside the function body on every invocation. CLAUDE.md Performance Standards says 'Hoist regex compile, JSON/date parsing, and DB/API calls out of any loop' — the spirit applies to repeated imports in hot paths. Move to module-level import (or justify with a circular-import comment).

_build_enrich_messages is missing the vocabulary normalization 'Instruction' block that anthropic_adapter._build_enrich_prompt appends (anthropic_adapter.py:353-360) — the explicit 'use Traditional Chinese names exactly as they appear in the reference list' directive. Currently latent because HybridLLMAdapter routes enrich_shop to Anthropic, but .env.example already documents LLM_PROVIDER=openai as a future option, so this will silently produce degraded vocab alignment once that path exists.

All OpenAI ChatCompletion calls use max_completion_tokens unconditionally. The design doc (docs/designs/2026-04-10-hybrid-llm-routing-design.md, Tool-Use Translation table row 6) explicitly notes max_completion_tokens is reasoning-model-only — non-reasoning GPT models may need max_tokens. Worth a comment explaining the choice, or guard with a model-capability check, so future model changes don't silently fail.

socket: str | None widened to str | bool | None without a corresponding test or plan-doc mention. Scope creep on a PR whose stated goal is 'Hybrid LLM routing implementation'. If the change was triggered by real scraper data, document it in the PR description or move to a separate commit/ticket.

skipped.reason: TYPE_CHECKING-guarded imports are safe here because 'from __future__ import annotations' is set — runtime annotations are strings.
test_llm_factory_returns_anthropic not setting mock.anthropic_classify_model is pre-existing test style and not touched by this PR.
summarize_reviews 0-indexed enumerate with i+1 offset vs Anthropic's enumerate(texts, 1) — both produce 1-indexed output; cosmetic difference only.
Missing @pytest.mark.asyncio decorators — backend uses asyncio_mode=auto in pyproject.toml, so bare async tests are picked up automatically.

codex_status: Adversarial Review (Codex): failed — skill 'codex:adversarial-review' unavailable in this environment
